_disclaimer: Internal development diagnostic asset for the ReTrace Stage A/B Ambiguity-and-Scope feasibility gate.
NOT an official benchmark. NOT a final evaluation. NOT human-validated until review_status changes per case.
Replay/mock execution is for runner correctness; any live execution is exploratory development-only.

cases.rationale: Explicit confirmed employer change with a grounded replacement belief.
cases.query: Where does the user currently work?

evidence_context.text: I work as a backend engineer at Acme Corp.
I formally accepted a senior engineer offer at Nimbus Labs and my last day at Acme Corp was last Friday.

candidate_beliefs.proposition: The user works at Acme Corp as a backend engineer.

candidate_replacement_beliefs.proposition: The user works at Nimbus Labs as a senior engineer.

b_emp_acme.rationale: Explicit employer change confirmed by accepted offer and last day.

stage_b_mock_verdicts.rationale: User has confirmed they no longer work at Acme Corp.

cases.rationale: Explicit phone model replacement with a grounded replacement belief.
cases.query: Which phone model does the user use day to day?

evidence_context.text: My daily phone is a Pixel 6 and I have used it for two years.
I traded in the Pixel 6 today and switched to a Pixel 9 Pro as my only daily device.

candidate_beliefs.proposition: The user's primary phone is a Pixel 6.

candidate_replacement_beliefs.proposition: The user's primary phone is a Pixel 9 Pro.

b_phone_pixel6.rationale: Explicit trade-in and switch to Pixel 9 Pro.

stage_b_mock_verdicts.rationale: Pixel 6 has been traded in.

cases.rationale: Confirmed relocation supersedes the prior residence city belief.
cases.query: Which city does the user live in?

evidence_context.text: I rent a flat in central Madrid and have lived here for four years.
I signed a lease in Berlin and moved last weekend; I am no longer in Madrid.

candidate_beliefs.proposition: The user lives in Madrid.

stage_b_mock_verdicts.rationale: User has moved out of Madrid to Berlin.

cases.rationale: Confirmed vehicle replacement supersedes the prior daily-driver belief.
cases.query: Which car does the user drive daily?

evidence_context.text: My daily driver is a 2014 Honda Civic.
I sold the Civic and just took delivery of a 2025 Hyundai Ioniq 6, which is now my only daily driver.

candidate_beliefs.proposition: The user's daily driver is a 2014 Honda Civic.

candidate_replacement_beliefs.proposition: The user's daily driver is a 2025 Hyundai Ioniq 6.

b_car_civic.rationale: Civic sold and replaced by Ioniq 6 as sole daily driver.

stage_b_mock_verdicts.rationale: User has sold the Civic and replaced it.

cases.rationale: Explicit mobility-blocking event invalidates the cycling-mobility condition.
cases.query: How does the user commute right now?

evidence_context.text: I commute to work by bicycle every weekday.
I fractured my left tibia yesterday and the orthopedist put me in a hard cast for eight weeks; no cycling allowed.

candidate_beliefs.proposition: The user commutes by bicycle.

candidate_conditions_by_belief.text: The user has lower-limb mobility sufficient to ride a bicycle.

b_bike_commute.rationale: Hard cast for eight weeks explicitly precludes cycling.

stage_b_mock_verdicts.rationale: Cast prevents cycling for the next eight weeks.

cases.rationale: License suspension blocks the legal-driving condition required for the driving belief.
cases.query: Does the user drive to work right now?

evidence_context.text: I drive my own car to work every weekday.
The DMV suspended my driver's license for six months starting today.

candidate_beliefs.proposition: The user drives their own car to work.

candidate_conditions_by_belief.text: The user holds a currently valid driver's license.

b_drives_to_work.rationale: DMV suspension removes the legal-driving prerequisite.

stage_b_mock_verdicts.rationale: User cannot legally drive while license is suspended.

cases.rationale: Expired gym membership blocks the membership-validity condition for ongoing gym attendance.
cases.query: Can the user enter their usual gym this week?

evidence_context.text: I attend my CrossFit gym four times per week.
My CrossFit membership expired last Friday and I have not renewed it; the gym has blocked my access until I do.

candidate_beliefs.proposition: The user attends their CrossFit gym multiple times per week.

candidate_conditions_by_belief.text: The user holds an active CrossFit gym membership.

b_attends_crossfit.rationale: Expired and unrenewed membership prevents gym access.

stage_b_mock_verdicts.rationale: User cannot enter the gym while membership is expired.

cases.rationale: Ear infection with explicit medical no-swim order blocks the medical-clearance condition.
cases.query: Can the user attend swim practice this week?

evidence_context.text: I swim with my masters team three mornings a week.
My ENT diagnosed a severe inner-ear infection and explicitly told me no swimming for at least three weeks.

candidate_beliefs.proposition: The user swims with a masters team multiple mornings each week.

candidate_conditions_by_belief.text: The user has medical clearance to swim.

b_swims_masters.rationale: ENT's explicit no-swimming order removes the medical-clearance prerequisite.

stage_b_mock_verdicts.rationale: ENT has explicitly prohibited swimming for at least three weeks.

cases.rationale: Cycling blocker must not erase an unrelated long-standing dietary preference.
cases.query: Summarize the user's daily commute and dietary preferences.

evidence_context.text: I have been vegetarian for six years and have no plans to change.
I cycle to work every day, even in winter.
I had knee surgery and I am on strict no-weight-bearing recovery for at least ten weeks; cycling is out of the question.

candidate_beliefs.proposition: The user commutes by bicycle.
The user follows a vegetarian diet.

candidate_conditions_by_belief.text: The user can place weight on their legs sufficiently to pedal a bicycle.

b_cycle_commute.rationale: Strict no-weight-bearing recovery for ten weeks prevents cycling.

stage_b_mock_verdicts.rationale: Knee surgery rules out cycling.
Dietary preference is unrelated to the injury.

cases.rationale: Job change must not erase an unrelated long-standing musical interest.
cases.query: Tell me about the user's job and music interests.

evidence_context.text: I have played jazz piano for fifteen years and still practice almost every day.
I currently work as a data analyst at Acme Corp.
I resigned from Acme and am between roles while job searching.

candidate_beliefs.proposition: The user works as a data analyst at Acme Corp.
The user plays jazz piano regularly.

candidate_replacement_beliefs.proposition: The user is between roles and is currently job searching.

b_job_acme_analyst.rationale: User has resigned and is now in a between-roles state.

stage_b_mock_verdicts.rationale: User no longer works at Acme.
Musical hobby is unrelated to the job change.

cases.rationale: Phone breakage blocks app-based contact but must not erase an unrelated language skill.
cases.query: What languages does the user speak and how can I reach them?

evidence_context.text: I am conversationally fluent in Japanese after living in Tokyo for two years.
Signal is my main messaging app for both work and friends.
My phone is completely broken and the repair shop will take at least ten days; I cannot use any apps until then.

candidate_beliefs.proposition: The user uses Signal as their main messaging app.
The user is conversationally fluent in Japanese.

candidate_conditions_by_belief.text: The user has a working phone that can run messaging apps.

b_uses_signal.rationale: Phone is broken for at least ten days.

stage_b_mock_verdicts.rationale: No working phone means Signal is currently unusable.
Language skill is unaffected by phone breakage.

cases.rationale: City relocation supersedes the residence belief but must not erase the family-relationship belief.
cases.query: Where does the user live and what are their family ties?

evidence_context.text: I am very close with my sister Aiko and we talk every week.
I live in Chicago, in the Logan Square neighborhood.
I just finished moving from Chicago to Seattle for a new job and signed the lease for a place in Capitol Hill.

candidate_beliefs.proposition: The user lives in Chicago.
The user is very close with their sister Aiko.

b_lives_in_chicago.rationale: Explicit move from Chicago to Seattle.

stage_b_mock_verdicts.rationale: User has moved to Seattle.
Family relationship is unrelated to relocation.

cases.rationale: A short-term schedule conflict should not erase a long-standing cycling preference.
cases.query: Does the user usually cycle to the office?

evidence_context.text: Cycling is my preferred daily commute; I have done it for three years.
I am traveling for client meetings this week, so I will not be cycling until Monday.

candidate_beliefs.proposition: The user prefers cycling as their daily commute.

stage_b_mock_verdicts.rationale: A short trip does not change a long-standing commuting preference.

cases.rationale: A single non-vegetarian meal does not erase a long-standing vegetarian preference.
cases.query: Is the user vegetarian?

evidence_context.text: I have been vegetarian for eight years and intend to stay vegetarian.
Tonight I am at a friend's birthday and there is only meat pizza, so I will eat one slice with pepperoni this once.

candidate_beliefs.proposition: The user follows a vegetarian diet as a long-standing preference.

stage_b_mock_verdicts.rationale: One-off exception at a party does not negate a long-standing preference.

cases.rationale: Working from a cafe for one day does not change a stated remote-work-from-home preference.
cases.query: Where does the user typically work from on weekdays?

evidence_context.text: I primarily work from my home office; I have done this for two years and it works well for me.
Internet is out at my apartment this morning, so I am working from the cafe down the street just for today.

candidate_beliefs.proposition: The user primarily works from their home office.

stage_b_mock_verdicts.rationale: A one-day location change due to outage is not a preference change.

cases.rationale: Taking a taxi once because of a train delay does not change a stated train-commute preference.
cases.query: How does the user commute to work most days?

evidence_context.text: I take the commuter train into the city every weekday and I find it reliable.
There is a signal failure and trains are suspended this morning; I took a taxi today as a one-off.

candidate_beliefs.proposition: The user commutes by train into the city on weekdays.

stage_b_mock_verdicts.rationale: A one-day taxi due to signal failure does not change the underlying commute habit.

cases.rationale: A current employment change must not erase a past academic accomplishment.
cases.query: What academic degree has the user earned?

evidence_context.text: I successfully defended my PhD in computational neuroscience in 2018.
I left research and now work as a product manager at a fintech startup.

candidate_beliefs.proposition: The user earned a PhD in computational neuroscience in 2018.

stage_b_mock_verdicts.rationale: A past degree is not invalidated by a later career change.

cases.rationale: A current relocation must not erase the user's birthplace fact.
cases.query: Where was the user born?

evidence_context.text: I was born in São Paulo, Brazil and spent my childhood there.
I recently relocated to Berlin for a new job and signed a long lease here.

candidate_beliefs.proposition: The user was born in São Paulo, Brazil.

stage_b_mock_verdicts.rationale: Birthplace is a historical fact that relocation does not change.

cases.rationale: A coaching role this season must not erase a past championship win.
cases.query: What major sporting accomplishment has the user achieved?

evidence_context.text: I won the state amateur tennis championship in 2019.
I currently coach my child's junior tennis team and no longer compete myself.

candidate_beliefs.proposition: The user won the state amateur tennis championship in 2019.

stage_b_mock_verdicts.rationale: A current coaching role does not erase a prior championship win.

cases.rationale: Current surgical recovery must not erase the user's prior Boston Marathon completion.
cases.query: Has the user completed the Boston Marathon?

evidence_context.text: I completed the Boston Marathon in 2021 with a finish time of 3:14:08.
I am currently rehabilitating from ACL surgery and cannot run at all this year.

candidate_beliefs.proposition: The user completed the Boston Marathon in 2021.

stage_b_mock_verdicts.rationale: Current injury does not change the historical fact of completing the marathon.

cases.rationale: A serious but undecided possible move directly makes next-spring residence continuity uncertain without superseding the current residence.
cases.query: Will the user still live in San Francisco next spring?

evidence_context.text: My lease in San Francisco runs through next spring, and I expect to remain here during that period.
My partner and I are seriously considering moving to Lisbon next spring, but nothing is decided and no lease has been signed.

candidate_beliefs.proposition: The user will still live in San Francisco next spring.

b_sf_next_spring.rationale: The possible Lisbon move directly affects next-spring residence continuity, but no decision or lease confirms a change.

stage_b_mock_verdicts.rationale: Next-spring residence is genuinely uncertain because a move is seriously considered but undecided.

cases.rationale: The possible job search directly affects future employer continuity but does not establish a current or future switch.
cases.query: Will the user still work at Tinker Industries after the summer?

evidence_context.text: I work as a designer at Tinker Industries and expect to stay there at least through the summer.
I am considering switching jobs because of the long commute, but I have not interviewed anywhere yet.

candidate_beliefs.proposition: The user will still work as a designer at Tinker Industries after the summer.

b_tinker_after_summer.rationale: Considering a job switch directly bears on future employment continuity but is not decisive.

stage_b_mock_verdicts.rationale: Future employment continuity is uncertain because a switch is under consideration but no interview or offer exists.

cases.rationale: A possible vegan trial directly makes future diet continuation uncertain while leaving the current diet intact.
cases.query: Will the user continue their mixed diet next month?

evidence_context.text: I eat a typical mixed diet including meat, fish and vegetables, and I expect to keep eating this way next month.
I have been reading about plant-based diets and I might try going fully vegan next month, but I have not started yet.

candidate_beliefs.proposition: The user will continue their typical mixed diet next month.

b_mixed_diet_next_month.rationale: The possible vegan trial directly affects next-month diet continuity, but the user has not started.

stage_b_mock_verdicts.rationale: Next-month diet continuation is uncertain because the user might try veganism but has not begun.

cases.rationale: Thinking about quitting because of knee pain directly makes future hiking continuation uncertain without proving the habit has ended.
cases.query: Will the user keep hiking every weekend next month?

evidence_context.text: I hike every weekend on the trails near my house and planned to keep that routine next month.
I am thinking about quitting hiking because my knees ache, but I have not made up my mind.

candidate_beliefs.proposition: The user will keep hiking every weekend next month.

b_hikes_next_month.rationale: Knee pain and possible quitting directly affect next-month hiking continuity but are undecided.

stage_b_mock_verdicts.rationale: Future hiking continuation is uncertain because the user is considering quitting but has not decided.

cases.rationale: A pending landlord dispute directly implicates next-month residence access but is not decisive enough to supersede or block it.
cases.query: Can the user rely on living in the Portland apartment next month?

evidence_context.text: I live in a rented apartment in Portland, Oregon, and my lease should let me stay there next month.
My landlord sent a confusing notice about possible building repairs next month, and I am waiting for clarification about whether tenants must temporarily leave.

candidate_beliefs.proposition: The user can rely on living in the Portland apartment next month.

b_pdx_next_month.rationale: The notice directly concerns next-month apartment availability, but its effect is unresolved.

stage_b_mock_verdicts.rationale: Next-month residence reliability is uncertain because repairs may require leaving, but clarification is pending.

cases.rationale: Persistent wrist pain directly implicates the planned chess league but is not yet a confirmed blocker.
cases.query: Can the user participate in next month's online chess league?

evidence_context.text: I play online chess almost every evening and planned to enter next month's online chess league.
Persistent wrist pain has stopped me playing this week; my doctor has scheduled tests and has not yet said whether I need to stop next month.

candidate_beliefs.proposition: The user can participate in next month's online chess league.

b_chess_league_next_month.rationale: Wrist pain directly affects chess participation, but medical guidance for next month is pending.

stage_b_mock_verdicts.rationale: League participation is uncertain because wrist pain blocks play this week but next-month status is unknown.

cases.rationale: Pending test results directly implicate near-future running safety but do not decisively block the routine.
cases.query: Can the user continue running three times a week next week?

evidence_context.text: I run three times a week along the river and planned to keep that schedule next week.
I am undergoing cardiac screening this week after some dizziness, and my doctor told me to wait for results before deciding how much exercise is safe.

candidate_beliefs.proposition: The user can continue running three times a week next week.

b_runs_next_week.rationale: Screening and medical uncertainty directly affect running safety, but there is no final instruction.

stage_b_mock_verdicts.rationale: Next-week running is uncertain pending test results and medical guidance.

cases.rationale: An unresolved contract renewal directly implicates future employment continuity but does not prove departure.
cases.query: Will the user still work at Kibo Systems next quarter?

evidence_context.text: I have been a project manager at Kibo Systems for almost three years, and my contract is expected to continue into next quarter.
Kibo has delayed my contract renewal decision, and HR said they cannot confirm next quarter yet.

candidate_beliefs.proposition: The user will still work as a project manager at Kibo Systems next quarter.

b_kibo_next_quarter.rationale: Contract renewal uncertainty directly affects next-quarter employment but is not a confirmed separation.

stage_b_mock_verdicts.rationale: Next-quarter employment is uncertain because renewal is delayed and unconfirmed.

cases.rationale: Knee injury blocks cycling commute but must not block adjacent cycling-related identity beliefs.
cases.query: Tell me about the user's cycling life right now.

evidence_context.text: I bought my carbon road bike four years ago and it still rides like new.
I live two blocks from a major protected bike path.
I follow a few professional cycling channels on YouTube every week.
I cycle to the office every weekday.
I tore my meniscus and my surgeon told me to stop cycling for the next four months.

candidate_beliefs.proposition: The user cycles to the office every weekday.
The user owns a carbon road bike.
The user lives near a protected bike path.
The user follows professional cycling channels on YouTube.

candidate_conditions_by_belief.text: The user has knee function sufficient for cycling.

b_cycle_commute_scope.rationale: Meniscus tear precludes cycling for the next four months.

stage_b_mock_verdicts.rationale: Cycling is blocked by knee surgery.
Owning a bike is unaffected by injury.
Location is unaffected by injury.
Media-consumption habit is unaffected by injury.

cases.rationale: Broken stove blocks home-cooking activity but must not erase unrelated food-identity beliefs.
cases.query: Describe the user's food and cooking life right now.

evidence_context.text: I love Italian food and have eaten my way through most of the trattorias in town.
I collect chef cookbooks and have over forty on my shelves.
I follow a number of food bloggers daily on social media.
I cook dinner at home almost every night using my gas stove.
My gas stove is broken and will not be repaired for at least three weeks; I cannot cook at home until then.

candidate_beliefs.proposition: The user cooks dinner at home most nights.
The user loves Italian food.
The user collects chef cookbooks.
The user follows food bloggers on social media.

candidate_conditions_by_belief.text: The user has a working stove for cooking at home.

b_cooks_dinner_home.rationale: Stove broken for at least three weeks blocks home cooking.

stage_b_mock_verdicts.rationale: Broken stove prevents home cooking.
Food preference is unaffected by appliance breakage.
Owning cookbooks is unrelated to cooking right now.
Following food bloggers is unrelated to the stove.

cases.rationale: Phone breakage blocks a single app-based habit but must not erase adjacent fitness-identity beliefs.
cases.query: Tell me about the user's running life right now.

evidence_context.text: I train for marathons twice a year and have done four already.
I follow several elite running coaches on YouTube for training inspiration.
I log every training run with the Strava app on my phone.
My phone got water damage and is being repaired for two weeks; I cannot use the Strava app in the meantime.

candidate_beliefs.proposition: The user logs runs with the Strava app on their phone.
The user trains for marathons multiple times per year.
The user follows several running coaches on YouTube.

candidate_conditions_by_belief.text: The user has a working phone that can run Strava.

b_uses_strava.rationale: Phone repair will take two weeks.

stage_b_mock_verdicts.rationale: Without a phone, Strava cannot be used right now.
Marathon training is unrelated to the phone outage.
Following coaches is unrelated to the phone outage.

cases.rationale: A short travel ban blocks one travel-dependent activity but must not erase unrelated heritage and language beliefs.
cases.query: Tell me about the user's connection to Italy right now.

evidence_context.text: I speak Italian fluently thanks to my Italian grandparents.
My family heritage is Italian and I keep in close touch with cousins in Bologna.
I fly to Italy for a long vacation every summer.
Due to a passport renewal hold, I am explicitly unable to travel internationally for the next four months; the Italy trip is on hold.

candidate_beliefs.proposition: The user flies to Italy each summer for vacation.
The user speaks Italian fluently.
The user has Italian family heritage and ties to relatives in Bologna.

b_flies_to_italy.rationale: Passport hold removes international-travel ability for four months.

stage_b_mock_verdicts.rationale: Passport hold prevents the Italy trip for now.
Language skill is unrelated to passport hold.
Family heritage is unrelated to travel ability.
